results.text: Invokes the revise-docs review (loads audience rules and reviews docs) rather than only writing the marker
results.evidence: transcript: 'Rules loaded: base + default overlay'; CLAUDE-vs-README boundary applied (config note → CLAUDE.md ## Config; README left intact); outputs/CLAUDE.md confirms the substantive edit and git-log shows a real docs commit

results.text: Writes the review marker at $(git rev-parse --git-common-dir)/doc-sweep-revise-marker equal to the current HEAD commit
Makes exactly ONE commit containing all the doc changes (not one commit per file)
results.evidence: git-log.txt: 2 commits total (init + one doc commit); git-show-stat.txt: that commit has 3 files changed

results.text: Stages only documentation paths it edited and does NOT use git add -A / git add . (so unrelated in-flight working-tree changes are not swept into the doc commit)
results.evidence: git-status.txt post-commit shows src/app.js still modified/unstaged; git-show-stat.txt commit contains only CLAUDE.md + two README.md, not app.js

results.text: Advances the review marker to the new HEAD after committing
Determines no documentation updates are needed and makes no commit (does not create an empty commit)
results.evidence: git-log.txt has only the initial commit — no new/empty commit was created

results.text: Still advances the review marker to the current HEAD so a previously-blocked push can proceed
results.evidence: doc-sweep-revise-marker == HEAD (671b88d) despite no commit being made
